title: Photosynthesis
puzzle: How do plants turn light into stored energy?
insight: Photosynthesis captures light energy and uses it to build sugars from water and carbon dioxide, making plants major energy converters for Earth.
key_ideas: Sunlight powers chemical reactions in plant cells.
The process creates sugars used for growth and storage.
Oxygen is released as a byproduct.
mind_blower: Much of the food chain runs on a trick plants learned for bottling sunlight.
next_curiosity: If plants store solar energy so effectively, what limits how much they can convert?
narration_text: How do plants turn light into stored energy? Photosynthesis captures light energy and uses it to build sugars from water and carbon dioxide, making plants major energy converters for Earth. Sunlight powers chemical reactions in plant cells. The process creates sugars used for growth and storage. Oxygen is released as a byproduct. Much of the food chain runs on a trick plants learned for bottling sunlight. If plants store solar energy so effectively, what limits how much they can convert?
